name: post-ap

metadata.name: post-ap
metadata.summary: Project with code used to ease data manipulation tasks

documentation:
# Description

This project is used to carry out checks on Run3 data. Check [this](doc/install.md) for installation instructions
and for instructions on how to setup an environment to use this project.

## Specifying configuration for filtering and slimming 

For this to work, configs need to be uploaded to the grid with the scripts below. The scripts need
to know the place in the grid where the user LFNs live. 

Once the config files have been updated, they have to be uploaded to the grid with:

```bash
update_config -u 1 -p /path/to/config.yaml
```

The `-u` flag will update the config file if its LFN is already in the grid.
The `-p` flat passes the path to the config to be uploaded.

# Save lists of PFNs

The PFNs to be processed will be stored once with the AP api and will be read as package data when processing ntuples. 
The list of PFNs is created with, e.g.:

```bash
save_pfns -c dt_2024_turbo_comp
```

where `-c` will correspond to the config file.

# Submitting jobs

---
All the jobs below require code that lives in a virtual environment, there should be multiple versions of this
environment and the latest one should be obtained by running:

```bash
dirac-dms-user-lfns -w dcheck.tar -b /lhcb/user/${LXNAME:0:1}/$LXNAME/run3/venv
```
---

The instructions below need to be done outside the virtual environment in an environment with access to `dirac` and in the `post_ap_grid`
directory.

First run a test job with:

```bash
job_filter -d dt_2024_turbo -c comp -j 1211 -e 003 -m local -n test_flt
```

where `-j` specifies the number of jobs. For tests, this is the number of files to process, thus, the test job does only one file. 
The `-n` flag is the name of the job, for tests it will do/send only one job if either:

1. Its name has the substring `test`.
1. It is a local job.

Thus one can do local or grid tests running over a single file.

For real jobs:

```bash
job_filter -d dt_2024_turbo -c comp -j 200 -e 003 -m wms -n flt_001
```

# Downloading ntuples

A test would look like:

```bash
run3_download_ntuples -j flt_004 -n 3 [-d $PWD/files]
```

where:

`-j`: Is the name of the job, which has to coincide with the directory name, where the ntuples are in EOS, e.g. `/eos/lhcb/grid/user/lhcb/user/a/acampove/flt_004`.   
`-n`: Number of ntuples to download, if not pased, will download everything.    
`-d`: Directory where output ntuples will go, if not passed, directory pointed by `DOWNLOAD_NTUPPATH` will be used.


A real download would look like:

```bash
run3_download_ntuples -j flt_001 -m 40
```

Where `-m` denotes the number of threads used to download, `-j` the name of the job.

## Notes

- The downloads can be ran many times, if a file has been downloaded already, it will not be downloaded again.

# Linking and merging

Once the ntuples are downloaded these need to be linked and merged with:

```bash
link_merge -j flt_002 -v v1
```

where `-j` is the name of the job and the files are linked to a directory named as `-v v1`. For tests run:

```bash
link_merge -j flt_002 -d 1 -m 10 -v v1
```

which will do the same with at most `10` files, can use debug messages with `-l 10`.

# Making basic plots

For this run:

```bash
plot_vars -y 2024 -v v2 -c bukee_opt -d data_ana_cut_bp_ee:Data ctrl_BuToKpEE_ana_ee:Simulation
```

which will run the plotting of the variables in a config specified by `bukee_opt` where also axis, names, ranges, etc are
specified. This config is in `post_ap_data`.
The script above will overlay data and MC.

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._get_args
documentation: Parse arguments

documentation: Returns list of paths to ROOT files corresponding to a given job

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._split_paths
documentation:
Takes list of paths to ROOT files
Splits them into categories and returns a dictionary:

category : [path_1, path_2, ...]

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._truncate_paths
documentation: Will limit the number of paths in the values if Data.Max is larger than zero

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._info_from_path
documentation:
Will pick a path to a ROOT file
Will return tuple with information associated to file
This is needed to name output file and directories

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._info_from_mc_path
documentation: Will return information from path to file

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._info_from_data_path
documentation: Will get info from data path

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._get_proc_evt
documentation:
Will load and return dictionary containing
{event_type : process}

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._kind_from_decay
documentation:
Will take string symbolizing decay
Will return kind of sample associated, e.g. analysis, calibration, same sign...

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._link_paths
documentation:
Makes symbolic links of list of paths of a specific kind
info is a tuple with = (sample, channel, kind, year) information
Will return directory where linked files are

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._do_link_paths
documentation:
Will check if target link exists, will delete it if it does
Will make link

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._merge_paths
documentation: Merge ROOT files of a specific kind

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._delete_tmp_files
documentation:
Delete files in /tmp that TFileMerger makes
In order to prevent storage to run out

functions.name: post_ap_scripts.link_merge._save_summary
documentation: Make text file with summary of file, e.g. 2024.root -> 2024.txt

modules.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter
documentation: Module with definition of ntuple_filter class

classes.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter

methods.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
---------------------
dataset: Dataset used, e.g. dt_2024_turbo
cfg_ver: Type of configuration, e.g. comp (comparison)
index  : Index of subsample to process, they start at zero up to ngroup - 1
ngroup : Number of groups into which to split filter

methods.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter._set_paths
documentation:
Loads dictionary with:

kind_of_file -> [PFNs]

correspondence

methods.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter._reformat
documentation:
Takes dictionary:

sample_kind -> [PFNs]

Returns dictionary

PFN -> sample_kind

Plus remove commas, etc from sample_kind

methods.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter._get_group
documentation:
Takes a dictionary mapping:

PFN -> sample_kind

and the total number of PFNs. Returns same dictionary for ith group out of ngroups

methods.name: post_ap.ntuple_filter.ntuple_filter.filter
documentation: Runs filtering

Class used to filter ntuples from analysis productions. Filtering means:
1. Picking a subset of the trees.
2. Picking a subset of the branches.

modules.name: post_ap.selector
documentation: Module containing selector class

classes.name: post_ap.selector.selector

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector.__init__
documentation:
rdf    : ROOT dataframe
cfg_nam: Name without extension of toml config file
is_mc  : MC or real data?

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector._set_process
documentation:
Will set the attribute self._proc based on the name of the HLT line in the dataframe
The attribute is used to apply a selection based on the config files content

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector._apply_selection
documentation:
Loop over cuts and apply selection
Save intermediate dataframes to self._d_rdf
Save final datafrme to self._rdf

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector._fix_bkgcat
documentation:
If data, will set cut to (1).
If MC, will find BKGCAT branch in dataframe (e.g. Lb_BKGCAT)
Will rename BKGCAT in cuts dictionary, such that truth matching cut can be applied

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector._get_bkgcat_name
documentation:
Will return name of branch in tree, holding the background category for the B meson, i.e.:

X_BKGCAT

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector._prescale
documentation: Will pick up a random subset of entries from the dataframe if 'prescale=factor' found in selection section

methods.name: post_ap.selector.selector.run
documentation:
Will return ROOT dataframe(s)

Parameters
-------------------
as_cutflow (bool): If true will return {cut_name -> rdf} dictionary
with cuts applied one after the other. If False (default), it will only return
the dataframe after the full selection
Class used to apply selections to ROOT dataframes

documentation: Module containing FilterFile class

classes.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile.dump_contents
documentation: Flag indicating if a text file with the file contents will be saved or only the ROOT file

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._check_mcdt
documentation: Will set self._is_mc flag based on config name

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._get_names_from_config
documentation: Will return all the HLT line names from config

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._set_tree_names
documentation: Will set the list of line names `self._l_line_name`

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._keep_branch
documentation: Will take the name of a branch and return True (keep) or False (drop)

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._get_column_names
documentation: Takes dataframe, returns list of column names as strings

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._rename_kaon_branches
documentation: Will define K_ = H_ for kaon branches. K_ branches will be dropped later

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._rename_mapped_branches
documentation: Will define branches from mapping in config. Original branches will be dropped later

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._define_branches
documentation:
Will take dataframe and define columns if "define" field found in config
Returns dataframe

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._define_heads
documentation:
Will take dataframe and define columns starting with head in _l_head to B_
Returns dataframe

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._define_head
documentation: Will define list of columns with a target head (e.g. B_some_name) from some original head (e.g. Lb_some_name)

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._get_rdf
documentation:
Will build a dataframe from a given HLT line and return the dataframe
_get_branches decides what branches are kept

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._wild_card_filter
documentation:
Takes list of branch names
removes only the ones matching wild card
returns remaining list

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._attach_branches
documentation:
Will check branches in rdf
Branches are dropped by:
    - keeping branches in _keep_branch function
    - Removing wildcarded branches in _wild_card_filter functio

line_name used to name file where branches will be saved.

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._tree_name_from_line_name
documentation:
Given a line name, it will check the config file to return KEE or KMM
to decide where the tree will be saved.

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile._save_file
documentation: Will save all ROOT dataframes to a file

methods.name: post_ap.filter_file.FilterFile.run
documentation: Will run filtering of files
Class used to pick a ROOT file path and produce a smaller version
